revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:浜松八幡宮]] {{Expand Japanese|浜松八幡宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hamamatsu Hachimangu | native_name = 浜松八幡宮 | image = Hamamatsu Hachiman Gu.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hamamatsu Hachimangu | coordinates = {{coord|34.715798|137.736743}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hachiman|lt=Hachiman|ar|هاتشيمان|az|Haçiman|ca|Hachiman|de|Hachiman|el|Χατσίμαν|en|Hachiman|es|Hachiman|eu|Hachiman|fa|هاچیمان|fi|Hachiman|fr|Hachiman|hu|Hacsiman|id|Hachiman|it|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|ko|하치만 신|mni|ꯍꯆꯤꯃꯥꯟ|my|ဟချိမန်း|pl|Hachiman|pt|Hachiman|ru|Хатиман|simple|Hachiman|sl|Hačiman|sv|Hachiman|tr|Hachiman|uk|Хачіман|uz|Hachiman|vi|Hachiman|wuu|八幡神|zh|八幡神|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hamamatsu Hachimangu'''|浜松八幡宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11642130|ltq=Q11642130|qid=Q11642130|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 7 ** P155: {{ill|Q28682729|ltq=Q28682729|qid=Q28682729|1=Kisa Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Tsumorino Shrine|lt=Tsumorino Shrine|qid=Q135039584|1=Tsumorino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q117084743|ltq=Q117084743|qid=Q117084743|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}} * {{ill|Q1048864|ltq=Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}} * {{ill|Fuchi district|lt=Fuchi district|fa|ناحیه فوچی|ja|敷知郡|qid=Q11499717|1=Fuchi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.715798|137.736743}} == image (P18) == * Hamamatsu Hachiman Gu.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q261637|ltq=Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181493 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 430-0918https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1080405001080https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県浜松市中央区八幡町2', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:浜松八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q119929629}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hamamatsu Hachimangu |画像 = [[File:Hamamatsu Hachiman Gu.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu)|Q117084743|qid=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}Hachiman-chō 2 |位置 = {{coord|34|42|57.4|N|137|44|12.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|Q11642130|qid=Q11642130|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} |創建 = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}[http://www.hamamatsuhachimangu.org/about/ Enshrined Deities / History] - Hamamatsu Hachimangu |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = August 14-15[http://www.hamamatsuhachimangu.org/schedule/ Main annual festivals and events] - Hamamatsu Hachimangu |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Hamamatsu
Hachimangu|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Hamamatsu Hachimangu''' (はままつはちまんぐう) is a shrine in Hachiman-chō, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu)|Q117084743|qid=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its origin is said to be '''Kobe Shrine''' (こべじんじゃ), a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11642130|qid=Q11642130|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} of Tōtōmi Province[http://www.hamamatsuhachimangu.org/ Hamamatsu Hachimangu]. === Overview === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, to pacify {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|lt=Totoumi|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, deity of maritime transport, was enshrined in Kobe-no-sato (around present {{ill|lt=Ozawatari-chō|Q109362601|qid=Q109362601|1=Ozawatari-chō}} in {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Hamamatsu)|Q117084743|qid=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}), and in the Heian-period {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it appears as Kobe Shrine. In {{ill|lt=938|Q24794|qid=Q24794|1=938}} (Tengyō 1), by divine oracle it was moved to the present site. In {{ill|lt=1051|Q19363|qid=Q19363|1=1051}} (Eishō 6), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|qid=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} (Hachiman Tarō) invited the two Hachiman deities ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}), and it came to be widely revered by warriors and commoners. In {{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|qid=Q6659|1=1570}} (Genki 1), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} moved his castle from {{ill|lt=Okazaki Castle|Q1013445|qid=Q1013445|1=Okazaki Castle}} to {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu Castle|Q1152352|qid=Q1152352|1=Hamamatsu Castle}} and used this Hachimangu as a ward against the {{ill|lt=kimon|Q11673539|qid=Q11673539|1=Kimon}} direction. During his defeat in the {{ill|lt=Battle of Mikatagahara|Q690464|qid=Q690464|1=Battle of Mikatagahara}} in {{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|qid=Q6670|1=1572}} (Genki 3), Ieyasu is said to have fled to this shrine and hidden in the hollow of a camphor tree before the shrine, escaping Takeda troops. Since auspicious clouds ({{ill|lt=saigun|Q1328219|qid=Q1328219|lt=zuigun|1=Cloud iridescence}}) rose from that camphor then, it came to be called "Kumotachi no Kusu" (Cloud-Rising Camphor)[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/miryoku/hakken/tanbo/201506-1.html Hamamatsu Hachimangu] - Hamamatsu City[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137771.html Hamamatsu Hachimangu] - Shizuoka Shimbun. Thereafter Ieyasu designated the shrine as a prayer site for successive Tokugawa generations, dedicating flags, bows, and sacred horses; while he resided in Edo, he sent representatives to worship. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}) === Precinct shrines === Sessha * Tōshō-gū - deity: {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, annual festival: April 17. Massha * Hamamatsu Inari Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}; annual festival: February 11. === Precinct features === * Kumotachi no Kusu * Satsusatsu no Matsu * Poem monument of Matsushima Jikko === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}} - {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|qid=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|qid=Q2156|1=January 3}} - {{ill|lt=Genshi-sai|Q11387821|qid=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} * {{ill|lt=January 28|Q2285|qid=Q2285|1=January 28}} - {{ill|lt=Otakiage|Q28687722|qid=Q28687722|lt=Shōnō-sai|1=Shōnō-sai}} * {{ill|lt=February 2|Q2313|qid=Q2313|1=February 2}} - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuina-shiki|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}} - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Setsubun-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|qid=Q2335|1=February 11}} - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} / {{ill|lt=Kigensetsu|Q11604982|qid=Q11604982|lt=Kenkoku-sai|1=Kigensetsu}} / Hamamatsu Inari Shrine annual festival * April 17 - Tōshō-gū annual festival * {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} - {{ill|lt=Shōwa Day|Q1361434|qid=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa-sai|1=Shōwa Day}} * {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}} - Summer {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōharae-shiki|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chinowa|1=UNKNOWN}} rite * August 14-15 - Annual Grand Festival ** 14th - Evening festival / dedicated {{ill|lt=tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|qid=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}} ** 15th - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikoshi procession|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Hōjōe|Q11078912|qid=Q11078912|1=Life release}} / i-matsuri * Mid-November - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}} - {{ill|lt=Ōmisoka|Q2357104|qid=Q2357104|1=Ōmisoka}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōharae-shiki|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Joya|Q7245702|qid=Q7245702|1=Chinese New Year's Eve}} festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly festival === Access === === Rail === * {{ill|lt=Enshū Railway|Q9286431|qid=Q9286431|1=Enshu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hachiman Station (Shizuoka)|Q5637051|qid=Q5637051|lt=Hachiman Station|1=Hachiman Station}}, 1 minute on foot * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Central|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu Station|Q603640|qid=Q603640|1=Hamamatsu Station}}, 10 minutes on foot === Bus === * {{ill|lt=Entetsu Bus|Q11642448|qid=Q11642448|1=Entetsu Bus}} bound for Sōde, get off at Hachimangū stop, 1 minute on foot === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.hamamatsuhachimangu.org/ Hamamatsu Hachimangu] - official site == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hamamatsu Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hamamatsu Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo Ward, Hamamatsu City]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]]
